verdicts.title: Cookie value used in cache filename — preg_replace strips dangerous chars
verdicts.reason: The taint flow ends at preg_replace('/[^a-z0-9_\-\=]/i', '-', $_cache_key) which sanitises the value to alphanumeric+hyphen+equals only, then uses it as a cache filename component. This is not RCE. The scanner incorrectly flagged preg_replace as a dangerous sink.

verdicts.title: Cookie value used in cache filename — same pattern as rce-002
verdicts.reason: Identical to rce-002. $_COOKIE value is stripped to alphanumeric chars and used as part of a cache filename. Not RCE.

verdicts.title: $_GET value used in cache filename — same preg_replace sanitisation
verdicts.reason: Same pattern as rce-002/003. $_GET value is sanitised via preg_replace to alphanumeric+hyphen+equals and appended to a cache filename. Not RCE.

verdicts.title: REQUEST_URI in preg_replace — URL canonicalization, not RCE
verdicts.reason: wpo_is_canonical_redirection_needed() takes parse_url(REQUEST_URI, PHP_URL_PATH) and uses preg_replace to append a trailing slash. The sink is a string manipulation used for a redirect-needed check, not shell execution.

verdicts.title: REQUEST_URI / HTTP_HOST → preg_replace for URL path normalization
verdicts.reason: wpo_get_url_path() applies preg_replace to normalise the path component of the URL (e.g., replacing index.php). The sink is pure string manipulation. No shell execution, no eval, not RCE.

verdicts.title: Same as rce-006 — second preg_replace in wpo_get_url_path()
verdicts.reason: Duplicate of rce-006, second preg_replace call in the same path-normalisation function. Not RCE.

verdicts.title: REQUEST_URI → preg_replace whitelist in wpo_current_url()
verdicts.reason: wpo_current_url() first tries filter_var(FILTER_VALIDATE_URL) and falls back to preg_replace('/[^a-z0-9\-._~:\/?#\[\]@!\'*+,;=%]/i', '', $url) — a whitelist allowlist. The result is a sanitised URL string stored in a static, not passed to shell. Not RCE.

verdicts.title: REQUEST_URI → preg_replace stripping query string
verdicts.reason: preg_replace('/\?.*/', '', $url) removes the query string for URL comparison. Pure string manipulation, not RCE.

verdicts.title: REQUEST_URI → preg_replace for domain stripping
verdicts.reason: preg_replace('#^(http|https):\/\/.*\/#Ui', '/', $url) strips the domain part to get a relative path. Pure string manipulation used for URL exception matching. Not RCE.

verdicts.title: REQUEST_URI → wpo_normalize_path() — deduplicate slashes
verdicts.reason: Long chain ending at preg_replace('|(?<=.)/+|', '/', $path) which collapses multiple slashes. This is wpo_normalize_path(), a filesystem path normalisation utility. Not RCE.

verdicts.title: $_SERVER value → wpo_early_sanitize_superglobal_text() — ends with htmlspecialchars
verdicts.reason: The function strips control chars with preg_replace then applies htmlspecialchars(). The scanner marks the preg_replace as a dangerous sink, but the returned value is HTML-safe. Not RCE.

verdicts.title: HTTP_HOST → wpo_current_url() — same as rce-008
verdicts.reason: HTTP_HOST flows into the same wpo_current_url() function already covered in rce-008. Sanitised by whitelist preg_replace. Not RCE.

verdicts.title: get_option('wpo_cache_config') → array_map('trim', ...) — not RCE
verdicts.reason: array_map('trim', $config[$field]) trims whitespace from config arrays. This is the classic false positive pattern explicitly noted in the review guidelines. Not RCE.

verdicts.title: intval(get_option) → array_map('trim', ...) — not RCE
verdicts.reason: Source is already intval()-cast. Downstream array_map('trim', ...) is not RCE. FALSE POSITIVE as per known FP patterns.

verdicts.title: compression_server option → call_user_func — hardcoded class whitelist
verdicts.reason: get_associated_task($server) only returns values from the hardcoded get_allowed_services() array: ['resmushit' => 'Re_Smush_It_Task']. If $server is not in the array, it falls back to the single default. The DB option cannot inject arbitrary class names. Not RCE.

verdicts.title: Same as rce-016 — compress_single_image() call path
verdicts.reason: Identical class-whitelist protection via get_associated_task(). Not RCE.

verdicts.title: Same as rce-016 — smush_image() call path
verdicts.reason: Same hardcoded whitelist in get_associated_task(). Not RCE.

verdicts.title: compression_server → call_user_func([task, 'is_server_online']) — whitelist protected
verdicts.reason: get_associated_task() returns only the single whitelisted class Re_Smush_It_Task. call_user_func invokes a static method on a known class. Not exploitable.

verdicts.title: attachment_id from task DB → preg_replace for relative path stripping — not RCE
verdicts.reason: attachment_id comes from task options stored in DB (set at queue creation by admin). File path is obtained via get_attached_file() (WP media library). preg_replace() at sink simply strips the uploads base dir prefix to get a relative path. No shell execution.

verdicts.title: backup_prefix option → preg_replace for path normalization — not RCE
verdicts.reason: backup_prefix is appended to a filename with basename() stripping directory traversal, then the relative path is computed via preg_replace to strip the uploads dir prefix. No shell execution.

verdicts.title: call_user_func($sanitisation, ...) in fetch_superglobal() — caller-supplied sanitiser is not user-controlled
verdicts.reason: $sanitisation is the 4th parameter of the internal fetch_superglobal() function. All call-sites pass hard-coded function names like 'sanitize_key', 'sanitize_text_field', etc. The parameter cannot be influenced by HTTP input. Not RCE.

verdicts.title: array_map('trim', get_option('wpo_min_disable_on_url')) — not RCE
verdicts.reason: array_map('trim', ...) is explicitly listed as a known false-positive pattern in the review guidelines. Not RCE.

verdicts.title: HTTP_HOST inside md5() → file_get_contents — path is md5 hash
verdicts.reason: The filename is constructed as: $cache_file_base . md5("$host-$request_uri..."). The HTTP_HOST is inside the md5() call, so it cannot influence the output to a predictable path or inject path separators. The resulting path is a fixed-length hex string. Not exploitable for file inclusion or traversal.

verdicts.title: Same as file-001 — HTTP_HOST inside md5() for cache header file
verdicts.reason: Same as file-001, different cache file extension. md5() prevents any meaningful taint propagation.

verdicts.title: $_GET → wpo_rest_cache_filename() → readfile($path) — sanitised to alphanumeric
verdicts.reason: wpo_rest_cache_filename() applies preg_replace('/[^a-z0-9_\-]/i', '-', ...) to all key-value pairs from $_GET, stripping all path separators and special chars. The resulting filename is prepended with WPO_CACHE_FILES_DIR. Path traversal is not possible because '/' and '.' are replaced with '-'. Not exploitable.

verdicts.title: $_GET → wpo_rest_cache_filename() → file_get_contents($headers_file) — same as file-003
verdicts.reason: Same path construction as file-003 with '.headers' appended. Cannot traverse directories. Not exploitable.

verdicts.title: get_post_meta('original-file') → realpath() → copy() — requires admin auth
verdicts.reason: Source is post meta 'original-file' written only by the plugin itself (backup_original_image writes it). realpath() resolves symlinks and verifies existence. The copy() overwrites the current image with its own backup. Requires authenticated admin to trigger smush. No attacker-controlled path traversal vector.

verdicts.title: attachment_id → get_attached_file() → copy() — internal file operation, admin only
verdicts.reason: attachment_id comes from task options set by authenticated admin. get_attached_file() looks up WP media library. copy() backs up within uploads dir. validate_file() checks extension. No user-controllable path traversal.

verdicts.title: attachment_id → get_attached_file() → file_put_contents() — internal file write, admin only
verdicts.reason: Same as file-006. file_put_contents() writes the optimised image back to the original media file path. Requires admin auth. Not attacker-exploitable.

verdicts.title: api_endpoint option → wp_remote_post() — requires admin privileges to set
verdicts.reason: api_endpoint is a task option validated with filter_var(FILTER_VALIDATE_URL) at line 64. The task option is set at queue creation by an authenticated admin. An admin setting their own API endpoint to an internal URL would be a self-SSRF with no privilege escalation. Not a meaningful external vulnerability.

verdicts.title: $_REQUEST['page'] → echo — only echoes literal 'active' or ''
verdicts.reason: At line 16, the echo outputs a ternary: ($active_page === $menu['menu_slug']) ? 'active' : ''. The user-supplied $active_page is only used in a strict string comparison; it is never itself echoed. The output is always either the literal string 'active' or empty string.

verdicts.title: echo intval($smush_options['image_quality']) — intval output is numeric only
verdicts.reason: intval() always returns an integer. echo of an integer cannot produce XSS.

verdicts.title: echo intval($wpo_minify_options['cache_lifespan']) — same as xss-002
verdicts.reason: intval() cast prevents XSS. Not exploitable.

verdicts.title: echo get_option(...) ternary — only echoes literal 'checked="checked"' or ''
verdicts.reason: The echo outputs a ternary where both branches are hard-coded string literals. The DB option value controls which branch is taken, never directly echoed. Not XSS.

verdicts.title: wpo_rest_cache_filename($_GET) → gmdate() → header('Last-Modified:') — no redirect
verdicts.reason: The sink is header('Last-Modified: ' . gmdate('D, d M Y H:i:s', $modified_time) . ' GMT'). $modified_time comes from filemtime() cast to int. The $_GET value flows through wpo_rest_cache_filename() to a file path used only to read mtime; the header value is always a formatted date string. Not an open redirect.

verdicts.title: Same as open-001 — second Last-Modified header call
verdicts.reason: Same analysis as open-001. Not an open redirect.

semgrep_verdicts.reason: The query uses esc_sql($multisite_plugin_table_name) for the table name, followed by $wpdb->prepare() for the WHERE clause parameters. Properly protected.

Both wp_redirect() calls at lines 409 and 418 are preceded by wp_verify_nonce() checks (lines 405/412) which require the user to be authenticated and possess a valid nonce. The redirect target is constructed via add_query_arg() on the current URL, not from raw user input. Not an open redirect.
sha1() is used here for cache filename deduplication when the filename exceeds 240 chars (performance/filesystem use), not for password storage. Not a security issue.
Low confidence finding. Require admin auth to reach these code paths (smush manager). Likely admin-only query building. Low severity even if confirmed.
Admin-only command class; requires capability check upstream. $wpdb->query with table name variables typical for DB optimizer functionality.
Database introspection queries (SHOW TABLES, SHOW TABLE STATUS etc) using table name variables. Table names from WP DB prefix are not user-supplied. Admin-only functionality.
Orphaned tables management is admin-only. Table names come from WordPress db prefix enumeration, not user input.
All md5() usages are for cache key generation / filename deduplication, not authentication or integrity verification. Low risk informational finding.
Redirect target would need to be reviewed in context but minify redirect is typically to a plugin-generated URL, not raw user input.

key_findings: All 22 taint flows labeled 'rce' are false positives. The scanner incorrectly identifies preg_replace(), array_map('trim'), and call_user_func() with hardcoded class whitelists as RCE sinks.

All 4 'file_inclusion' critical/high flows are false positives: files-001/002 use md5() making path injection impossible; files-003/004 sanitise with whitelist regex; files-005/006/007 are admin-only internal file operations.
The single 'ssrf' finding (ssrf-001) requires admin privileges to set the api_endpoint option and validates with FILTER_VALIDATE_URL.
All 4 'xss' flows are false positives: xss-001 never echoes the user value (only uses it in a comparison); xss-002/003 use intval(); xss-004 echoes only literal strings.
Both 'open_redirect' flows are mislabelled — the actual sink is a Last-Modified HTTP header using a gmdate() integer value, not a Location redirect.
Semgrep SQL injection findings are all low-confidence, admin-only code paths using table names not derived from user input.
